tasks.title: Count Character Frequencies in a String
tasks.description:
When processing text data, it is often useful to know how often each character appears. This frequency information is used in cryptography, data compression, and text analysis. Understanding character distribution helps identify patterns and anomalies in written data.

Your program must read a single line of text and count how many times each alphabetic character (both uppercase and lowercase, treated as the same letter) appears in the string. Print each letter that appears at least once, in the order a–z, followed by its frequency. Non-alphabetic characters should be ignored.

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: "hello" → h: 1 → e: 1 → l: 2 → o: 1. Output: h 1 / e 1 / l 2 / o 1.

tasks.title: Reverse a String and Check if Palindrome
tasks.description:
A palindrome is a word or phrase that reads the same forwards and backwards, ignoring non-alphabetic characters and case. Palindromes appear in wordplay puzzles, license plate checks, and data validation. Detecting palindromes requires comparing characters from both ends of a string moving toward the center.

Your program must read a single line of text and determine whether it is a palindrome. Consider only alphabetic characters and treat uppercase and lowercase letters as equivalent. Print "yes" if the string is a palindrome, "no" otherwise.

test_cases.input: A man a plan a canal Panama

test_results.input: A man a plan a canal Panama

tasks.title: Find the Longest Word in a String
tasks.description:
Text processing often requires identifying the most prominent words by length. This operation is used in readability analysis, vocabulary assessment, and document summarization. Finding the longest word requires iterating through the string, identifying word boundaries, and tracking the maximum length encountered.

Your program must read a single line of text and find the longest word (a contiguous sequence of alphabetic characters). Print the longest word. If multiple words tie for longest length, print the first one encountered. Words are separated by non-alphabetic characters.

tasks.title: Count Vowels and Consonants
tasks.description:
Analyzing the vowel-to-consonant ratio in text is useful for linguistic studies, readability scoring, and phonetic analysis. Vowels and consonants form the building blocks of syllables and words. Distinguishing between them requires examining each alphabetic character against a known set.

Your program must read a single line of text and count how many vowels and how many consonants appear in it. A vowel is a, e, i, o, or u (case-insensitive). All other alphabetic characters are consonants. Non-alphabetic characters are ignored. Print two lines: the first with the count of vowels, the second with the count of consonants.

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: "aei" → vowels: 1 → 2 → 3. Output: vowels 3, consonants 0.

tasks.title: Remove Duplicate Characters from a String
tasks.description:
Removing duplicates from text is common in data cleaning, deduplication algorithms, and set operations. A string with duplicates removed shows each character at most once, preserving the order of first appearance. This operation is foundational to many string-processing utilities.

Your program must read a single line of text and output a new string containing each character exactly once, in the order it first appears. Non-alphabetic characters and whitespace should be treated as regular characters and also deduplicated.

tasks.title: Validate Balanced Parentheses
tasks.description:
Checking whether parentheses, brackets, and braces are correctly matched is fundamental to compiler design, syntax validation, and expression evaluation. A balanced sequence has each opening symbol paired with a corresponding closing symbol in the correct order. This check is performed millions of times daily in code editors and IDEs.

Your program must read a single line of text and check whether all parentheses ( ), brackets [ ], and braces { } are properly balanced and correctly nested. Other characters in the string should be ignored. Print "yes" if balanced, "no" otherwise.

tasks.title: Count Words Longer Than N Characters
tasks.description:
Text analysis often requires filtering words by length to understand vocabulary complexity and readability. Identifying which words exceed a certain length threshold helps in assessing document difficulty and audience suitability. This requires both word extraction and conditional counting.

Your program must read an integer N, then read a line of text, and count how many words in that text have more than N characters. A word is a contiguous sequence of alphabetic characters. Print the count of words exceeding N characters in length.

test_cases.input:
10
a b c de fgh ijkl

test_results.input:
10
a b c de fgh ijkl

tasks.title: Rotate Array Elements
tasks.description:
Array rotation is a fundamental operation in data structures and algorithms, used in circular buffers, image processing, and algorithm optimization. Rotating means shifting all elements by a fixed number of positions, with elements that fall off one end wrapping around to the other. Understanding rotation builds intuition for circular indexing and modular arithmetic.

Your program must read an integer N (the size of the array), then N integers, then an integer K (the rotation amount). Rotate the array to the right by K positions and print the resulting array. For example, rotating [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] right by 2 produces [4, 5, 1, 2, 3].

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], rotate right by 2 → [4, 5] at front, [1, 2, 3] at end. Output: 4 5 1 2 3.

tasks.title: Find Missing Number in Array
tasks.description:
When storing sequences of numbers, data loss or transmission errors can result in gaps. Finding which number is missing from a sequence is a common integrity check in databases, file transfers, and inventory systems. Efficient detection requires identifying what should be present and comparing against what is actually found.

Your program must read an integer N, then read N integers that form a sequence of numbers from 1 to N+1 with exactly one number missing. Find and print the missing number. You may assume all input numbers are distinct and within the range 1 to N+1.

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: 3 numbers [1, 2, 4] from range 1–4 → sum of input: 1 → 3 → 7, expected sum: 1 → 3 → 6 → 10. Output: 3.

tasks.title: Check if Two Strings are Anagrams
tasks.description:
Anagrams are words or phrases that contain the same characters in a different order. Anagram detection is used in word games, cryptanalysis, and plagiarism detection. Two strings are anagrams if each character appears the same number of times in both, regardless of order or case.

Your program must read two lines of text and determine whether they are anagrams of each other. Consider only alphabetic characters (treating uppercase and lowercase as equivalent) and ignore spaces and other non-alphabetic characters. Print "yes" if they are anagrams, "no" otherwise.
